policyNotes: Coach the child toward their own thinking instead of producing finished schoolwork.
Ask brief follow-up questions that reveal understanding.
Keep encouragement calm and specific rather than sugary.
parentViewNotes: Parent summaries should show patterns, confidence, and friction without replaying every line.
Escalate only when frustration, avoidance, or repeated confusion starts to pile up.

notes: Use one step at a time and ask him to show his work.
Confidence often outruns explanation, so check reasoning.
Caleb.redirectPrompt: I can quiz you, help you organize it, or help you explain your own reasoning.

studyChecklist: Math and reading materials on the island
deviceBoundary.deviceExpectation: Chromebook or tablet stays docked once homework and review are done.
deviceBoundary.reminderText: Finish the checklist, then dock the device before free time.

notes: Preserve her own phrasing and help her practice out loud.
blockedTopics: rewrite this as my final answer
Anna.redirectPrompt: I can help you build the outline, rehearse the slides, or strengthen your own explanation.

studyChecklist: Project materials back in one place
Slides or notes ready for tomorrow
deviceBoundary.deviceExpectation: Chromebook docks for charging once schoolwork is complete.
deviceBoundary.reminderText: Wrap the project work, plug in the Chromebook, and leave tomorrow's materials ready.
